http://purl.org/vocommons/voaf#Vocabular.label: Vocabulary
http://purl.org/vocommons/voaf#Vocabular.description: A vocabulary is a set of words, typically the set in a language or the set known to an individual.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: OperationalKPI
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The operational KPI is the KPI at the operational appliance level, the KPI corresponding to zone and equipment level.

https://w3id.org/seas/Room.label: Room
https://w3id.org/seas/Room.description: A room in a building space enclosed by surfaces, this could also be modelled as role of space, not subclass of the space itself.

https://w3id.org/saref#PowerUnit.label: Power unit
https://w3id.org/saref#PowerUnit.description: The unit of measure for power

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Sanita.label: Sanitary terminal
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Sanita.description: A sanitary terminal is a fixed appliance or terminal usually supplied with water and used for drinking, cleaning or foul water disposal or that is an item of equipment directly used with such an appliance or terminal.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Shadin.label: Shading device
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Shadin.description: Shading devices are purpose built devices to protect from the sunlight, from natural light, or screening them from view. Shading devices can form part of the facade or can be mounted inside the building, they can be fixed or operable.

https://w3id.org/respond#Dwelling.label: Dwelling
https://w3id.org/respond#Dwelling.description: Place where a person or group of people live.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: Pump
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The pump in HVAC system, DHW system, etc.

http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/Person.label: Person
http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/Person.description: It represents a single individual of any age and having any type of characteristics.

https://w3id.org/saref#TemperatureUnit.label: Temperature unit
https://w3id.org/saref#TemperatureUnit.description: The unit of measure for temperature

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: EnergyForecast
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The forecast of energy demand or production

https://w3id.org/seas/Lobby.label: Lobby
https://w3id.org/seas/Lobby.description: An entrance hall, corridor, or vestibule, as in a public building, often serving as an anteroom; foyer.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: PowerSystemResource
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Power system resource is the resource in district microgrid, including energy generation units, energy storage units, energy consumers and power delivery system, etc.

https://w3id.org/respond#AlarmProperty.label: Alarm
https://w3id.org/respond#AlarmProperty.description: A warning of danger or potential undesired situation.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Event.label: Event
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Event.description: An occurrence; something that happens (Oxford Dictionary of English). It represents when something planned or sudden happens.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: OccupantNumber
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The number of occupants in the zone or building

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Fire.label: Fire
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Fire.description: An event that may burn different parts of the building and hazourdous for both the people and the building.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/FlowIn.label: Flow instrument
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/FlowIn.description:
A flow instrument reads and displays the value of a particular property of a system at a point, or displays the difference in the value of a property between two points.

Instrumentation is typically for the purpose of determining the value of the property at a point in time. It is not the purpose of an instrument to record or integrate the values over time (although they may be connected to recording devices that do perform such a function). This entity provides for all forms of mechanical flow instrument (thermometers, pressure gauges etc.) and electrical flow instruments (ammeters, voltmeters etc.)

https://w3id.org/seas/NonResidentialBuil.label: Non-Residential Building
https://w3id.org/seas/NonResidentialBuil.description: A  non-residential building is a building at least half of which is used for other than residential purposes.

https://w3id.org/respond#NetworkLinkStre.label: Network Link Strength
https://w3id.org/respond#NetworkLinkStre.description: The strength of a network link

https://w3id.org/saref#TemperatureSensor.label: Temperature sensor
https://w3id.org/saref#TemperatureSensor.description: A device that consists of a sensor, has category saref:Sensor, performs the saref:SensingFunction and is used for the purpose of sensing a property of type saref:Temperature

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Sensor.label: Sensor
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Sensor.description: A sensor is a device that measures a physical quantity and converts it into a signal which can be read by an observer or by an instrument.

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.label: Transformer
https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: In electrical engineering, a transformer is a passive component that transfers electrical energy from one electrical circuit to another circuit, or multiple circuits.

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.label: Flow meter
https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: Flow measurement is the quantification of bulk fluid movement.

https://w3id.org/seas/Elevator.label: Elevator
https://w3id.org/seas/Elevator.description: Elevator is used to transport people between different floors.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: GlobalHorizontalSolar
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The intensity of global solar radiation in horizontal level

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Escalator.label: Escalator
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Escalator.description: A moving staircase consisting of an endlessly circulating belt of steps driven by a motor, which conveys people between the floors of a public building (Oxford Dictionary of English).

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: HVACSystem
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning (HVAC) system that provides building indoor comfort

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: OccupantBehavior
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The behavior of occupant that influences to energy demand

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: U-value
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: Overall thermal conductance of the envelope

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: Room
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: The room contained in the zone

https://w3id.org/sbeo#EmergencyEvacuatio.label: Emergency Evacuation Group
https://w3id.org/sbeo#EmergencyEvacuatio.description: A group of two or more persons who are supposed to evacuate the building together.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#FloorEntrance.label: Floor Entrance
https://w3id.org/sbeo#FloorEntrance.description: A logical point that is used to represent the entrance to a floor.

https://w3id.org/saref#State.label: State
https://w3id.org/saref#State.description: The state in which a device can be found, e.g, ON/OFF/STANDBY, or ONLINE/OFFLINE. We propose here a list of states that are relevant for the purpose of SAREF, but this list can be extended.

https://w3id.org/saref#Generator.label: Generator
https://w3id.org/saref#Generator.description: A type of energy-related device that generates energy

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Entrance.label: Entrance
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Entrance.description: Entrance represents a space or a door that is used to enter into a building. Normally, it is also used as an exit.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#AlzheimersDiseased.label: Alzheimer's Diseased Person
https://w3id.org/sbeo#AlzheimersDiseased.description: A person having an Alzheimer's disease.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#PassiveFireProtect.label: Passive Fire Protection
https://w3id.org/sbeo#PassiveFireProtect.description: It is an integral component installed in the building while construction to resist the fire.

https://w3id.org/saref#MeteringFunction.label: Metering function
https://w3id.org/saref#MeteringFunction.description: A function that allows to get data from a meter, such as current meter reading or instantaneous demand

http://purl.org/ontology/olo/core#Ordere.label: Ordered List
http://purl.org/ontology/olo/core#Ordere.description: An ordered list with a given length an indexed items.

https://w3id.org/seas/OfficeBuilding.label: Business Building
https://w3id.org/seas/OfficeBuilding.description: Places where services are provided. Examples: banks, insurance agencies.

https://w3id.org/saref#Load.label: Load
https://w3id.org/saref#Load.description: A type of energy-related device that consumes energy

https://w3id.org/sbeo#SensoryAbility.label: Sensor Ability
https://w3id.org/sbeo#SensoryAbility.description: Sensory ability is referred to the brain's ability to receive, interpret, and effectively use the information provided by the senses in human beings such as touch, hear, smell, vision, etc.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Buildi.label: Building
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Buildi.description: A building represents a structure that provides shelter for its occupants or contents and stands in one place. The building is also used to provide a basic element within the spatial structure hierarchy for the components of a building project (together with site, storey, and space).

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: District
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: District is the platform where contains multi buildings and other physical objects.

https://w3id.org/seas/InstitutionalBuild.label: Institutional Building
https://w3id.org/seas/InstitutionalBuild.description: Institutions such as hospitals providing medical and surgical treatment and nursing care for ill or injured people.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: Assumption
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The assumption of the methematical model of KPI calculation

https://w3id.org/saref#StartStopFunction.label: Start stop function
https://w3id.org/saref#StartStopFunction.description: An actuating function that allows to start and stop a device

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Burner.label: Burner
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Burner.description: A burner is a device that converts fuel into heat through combustion. It includes gas, oil, and wood burners.

https://w3id.org/saref#Service.label: Service
https://w3id.org/saref#Service.description: A service is a representation of a function to a network that makes the function discoverable, registerable, remotely controllable by other devices in the network. A service can represent one or more functions. A Service is offered by a device that wants (a certain set of) its function(s) to be discoverable, registerable, remotely controllable by other devices in the network. A Service must specify the device that is offering the service and the function(s) to be represented.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Protec.label: Protective device
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Protec.description:
A protective device breaks an electrical circuit when a stated electric current that passes through it is exceeded.

A protective device provides protection against electrical current only (not as a general protective device). It may be used to represent the complete set of elements including both the tripping unit and the breaking unit that provide the protection. This may be particularly useful at earlier stages of design where the approach to breaking the electrical supply may be determined but the method of tripping may not. Alternatively, this entity may be used to specifically represent the breaking unit alone (in which case the tripping unit will also be specifically identified). This entity is specific to dedicated protective devices and excludes electrical outlets that may have circuit protection.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/SpaceH.label: Space heater
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/SpaceH.description:
Space heaters utilize a combination of radiation and/or natural convection using a heating source such as electricity, steam or hot water to heat a limited space or area. Examples of space heaters include radiators, convectors, baseboard and finned-tube heaters.

UnitaryEquipment should be used for packaged units supporting a combination of heating, cooling, and/or dehumidification; Coil should be used for coil-based floor heating.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: Duct
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The duct in HVAC system

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.label: DC-DC
https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: A DC-to-DC converter is an electronic circuit or electromechanical device that converts a source of direct current (DC) from one voltage level to another.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#AppartmentBuilding.label: Appartment Building
https://w3id.org/sbeo#AppartmentBuilding.description: A set of one or more buildings that contain apartments in it.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#LowVisionPerson.label: Low Vision Person
https://w3id.org/sbeo#LowVisionPerson.description: A person whose visual acuity is 20/70 or poorer in the better-seeing eye and cannot be corrected or improved with regular eyeglasses.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Electr.label: Electric time control
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Electr.description: An electric time control is a device that applies control to the provision or flow of electrical energy over time.

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.label: Tank
https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: A tank is an armoured fighting vehicle intended as a primary offensive weapon in front-line ground combat.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#BuildingExit.label: Building Exit
https://w3id.org/sbeo#BuildingExit.description: A logical point that is used to represent the exit of a building.

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: BuildingStorey
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: Building storey captures the storey structure in building

https://w3id.org/sbeo#ColourBlindPerson.label: Colour Blind Person
https://w3id.org/sbeo#ColourBlindPerson.description: A person having the decreased ability to see color or differences in color.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: KPICalculationComponent
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The component needed for KPI calculation, like mathematical model, datum source, etc.

https://w3id.org/seas/Hall.label: Hall
https://w3id.org/seas/Hall.description: A large entrance room of a house or building.

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: Schedule
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: The day schedule of the event taken place in the zone

https://w3id.org/sbeo#ImpairedPerson.label: Impaired Person
https://w3id.org/sbeo#ImpairedPerson.description: An individual whose natural senses or physical abilities are either diminished or damaged.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: PowerDeliveryUnit
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Power devices that are capable of delivering electric power to the household

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Buildi.label: Building object
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Buildi.description: An object in the building that can be controlled by devices, such as a door or a window that can be automatically opened or closed by an actuator.  (Definition taken from SAREF 1.0)

https://w3id.org/sbeo#HotelBuilding.label: Hotel Building
https://w3id.org/sbeo#HotelBuilding.description: A building that contains rooms, apartments or suites where people stay for a specific time period.

https://w3id.org/respond#WeatherForecast.label: Weather Forecasting Service
https://w3id.org/respond#WeatherForecast.description: Represents a service that provides weather forecast to specific locations.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Impact.label: Impact
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Impact.description: An impact is a long-lasting effect of an event (or activity), that continues to exist after the event (or activity) has taken place.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/FlowMo.label: Flow moving device
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/FlowMo.description: The distribution flow element FlowMovingDevice defines the occurrence of an apparatus used to distribute, circulate or perform conveyance of fluids, including liquids and gases (such as a pump or fan), and typically participates in a flow distribution system.

https://w3id.org/saref#BuildingRelated.label: Building
https://w3id.org/saref#BuildingRelated.description: A category that includes devices as described by building related data models, such as IFC and FIEMSER

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: EnergySupplyFromSiteProduction
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The energy supplied directly by site production for end uses

https://w3id.org/saref#SmokeSensor.label: Smoke sensor
https://w3id.org/saref#SmokeSensor.description: A device that consists of a sensor, has category saref:Sensor, performs the saref:SensingFunction and saref:EventFunction (which notifies that a certain threshold has been exceeded), and is used for the purpose of sensing a property of type saref:Smoke.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#SpaceSafety.label: Space Saftey
https://w3id.org/sbeo#SpaceSafety.description: A parameter to express the safety of any space for a specific person (or type of persons) in terms of some value.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Electr.label: Electric flow storage device
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Electr.description: An electric flow storage device is a device in which electrical energy is stored and from which energy may be progressively released.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: KeyPerformanceIndicator
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Key Performance Indicator that measures the progress towards stakeholder's key performance goal

https://w3id.org/saref#Meter.label: Meter
https://w3id.org/saref#Meter.description: A device built to accurately detect and display a quantity in a form readable by a human being. Further, a device of category saref:Meter that performs a saref:MeteringFunction.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: Substation
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Substation is where transform the high/medio voltage power from main grid to low voltage for the energy use of district consumers.

https://w3id.org/saref#Time.label: Time
https://w3id.org/saref#Time.description: A saref:Property that allows to specify the time concept in terms of instants or intervals according to the imported W3C Time ontology.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Lamp.label: Lamp
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Lamp.description: A lamp is an artificial light source such as a light bulb or tube.

http://purl.oclc.org/NET/ssnx/ssn#Observ.label: Observation
http://purl.oclc.org/NET/ssnx/ssn#Observ.description:
The observation data of the sensor or meter, the measurement of different parameter
An Observation is a Situation in which a Sensing method has been used to estimate or calculate a value of a Property of a FeatureOfInterest. Links to Sensing and Sensor describe what made the Observation and how; links to Property and Feature detail what was sensed; the result is the output of a Sensor; other metadata details times etc.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#PointOfInterest.label: Point Of Interest
https://w3id.org/sbeo#PointOfInterest.description: Any physical or virtual location or object, which may be of interest to a user and may serve as a navigation destination (e.g., room, water dispenser, waiting zone, assembly point, shop, seat, etc).

https://w3id.org/saref#LightingDevice.label: Lighting device
https://w3id.org/saref#LightingDevice.description: A device used for illumination, irradiation, signaling, or projection

https://w3id.org/saref#StartState.label: Start state
https://w3id.org/saref#StartState.description: The state of a device that is STARTED

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: StoringUnit
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Storing unit is the device that stores energy.

https://w3id.org/seas/Attic.label: Attic
https://w3id.org/seas/Attic.description: the part of a building, especially of a house, directly under a roof; garret.

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: Shade
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: The shade of building envelope

https://w3id.org/respond#TemperatureSetp.label: Temperature Setpoint
https://w3id.org/respond#TemperatureSetp.description: The target value at which a controller attempts to maintain the temperature.

https://w3id.org/seas/LivingRoom.label: Living Room
https://w3id.org/seas/LivingRoom.description: Living Room is the main room of daytime activity.

https://w3id.org/saref#Humidity.label: Humidity
https://w3id.org/saref#Humidity.description: A saref:Property related to some measurements that are characterized by a certain value that is measured in a humidity unit

https://w3id.org/sbeo#TravelTime.label: Travel Time
https://w3id.org/sbeo#TravelTime.description: A parameter to express the time taken (in seconds) by a specific person (or type of persons) to go from one point to another point (i.e., Route Element).

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/FlowTe.label: Flow terminal
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/FlowTe.description: The distribution flow element FlowTerminal defines the occurrence of a permanently attached element that acts as a terminus or beginning of a distribution system (such as an air outlet, drain, water closet, or sink). A terminal is typically a point at which a system interfaces with an external environment.

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: Efficiency
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: Efficiency is the often measurable ability to avoid making mistakes or wasting materials, energy, efforts, money, and time while performing a task.

https://saref.etsi.org/core/Actuator.label: Actuator
https://saref.etsi.org/core/Actuator.description: A device responsible for moving or controlling a mechanism or system

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Switch.label: Switching device
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Switch.description:
A switch is used in a cable distribution system (electrical circuit) to control or modulate the flow of electricity.

Switches include those used for electrical power, communications, audio-visual, or other distribution system types as determined by the available ports.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Actuat.label: Actuator
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Actuat.description: An actuator is a mechanical device for moving or controlling a mechanism or system. An actuator takes energy, usually created by air, electricity, or liquid, and converts that into some kind of motion.

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: EnergyParameter
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: Energy parameter describes energy behavior in district and buildings, inluding energy production, energy storage, energy supply, energy demand, energy cost and tariff, etc.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#MotorImpairedPerso.label: Motor Impaired Person
https://w3id.org/sbeo#MotorImpairedPerso.description: A person who has either partial or total loss of function of a body part, usually a limb or limbs.

https://w3id.org/seas/DiningRoom.label: Dining Room
https://w3id.org/seas/DiningRoom.description: A room in which meals are eaten, as in a home or hotel, especially the room in which the major or more formal meals are eaten.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Physic.label: Physical object
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Physic.description: Any Object that has a proper space region.  (Definition extracted from DUL ontology)

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: Equation
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The equation of the mathematical model for KPI calculation

https://w3id.org/sbeo#HearingImpairedPer.label: Hearing Impaired Person
https://w3id.org/sbeo#HearingImpairedPer.description: It is a partial or total unability to hear. It is also known as Hearing loss.

https://w3id.org/respond#ElectricConsump.label: Power Consumption
https://w3id.org/respond#ElectricConsump.description: The rate, per unit time, at which energy is consumed.

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: Building
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: The building in the district.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#PhysicallyImpaired.label: Physically Impaired Person
https://w3id.org/sbeo#PhysicallyImpaired.description: A physical impairment is a condition in which any part of person's body is either  damaged or is not working properly.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/FireSu.label: Fire suppression terminal
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/FireSu.description:
A fire suppression terminal has the purpose of delivering a fluid (gas or liquid) that will suppress a fire.

A fire suppression terminal provides for all forms of sprinkler, spreader and other form of terminal that is connected to a pipework system and intended to act in the role of suppressing a fire.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#WaitingZone.label: Waiting Zone
https://w3id.org/sbeo#WaitingZone.description: A space (normally with seating), where people can wait to do some specific activity. During emergency condtions, it is also used for several purposes, such as unification of people with their family members, acquaintances or emergency rescue teams, etc.

https://w3id.org/saref#Switch.label: Switch
https://w3id.org/saref#Switch.description: A device of category saref:Actuator that performs an actuating function of type saref:OnOffFunction or saref:OpenCloseFunction

https://w3id.org/respond#HeatPump.label: Heat Pump
https://w3id.org/respond#HeatPump.description: A heat pump is a device that transfers heat energy from a source of heat to what is called a heat sink.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Congestion.label: Congestion
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Congestion.description: An event that happens at bottlenecks when the flow of people increases than a provided limit due to herding (and similar phenomena of human behaviour), especially during an emergency evacuation process.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: PhotovoltaicType
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Photovoltaic type describes the type of PV generating unit

https://w3id.org/sbeo#TurnPoint.label: Turn Point
https://w3id.org/sbeo#TurnPoint.description: A logic point that is used to enforce people to change their direction.

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.label: Current
https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: A property related to measurements measured in Ampere (A).

https://w3id.org/saref#Task.label: Task
https://w3id.org/saref#Task.description: The goal for which a device is designed (from a user perspective). For example, a washing machine is designed for the task of washing. We propose here a list of tasks that are relevant for the purpose of SAREF, but this list can be extended.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Datum source needed for KPI calculation

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: EquipmentParameter

https://w3id.org/sbeo#EmergencyExit.description: Emergency exit is logical point that is only used in case of any emergency conditions such as fire, etc.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: Battery
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The set of batteries that stores energy.

https://w3id.org/saref#Device.label: Device
https://w3id.org/saref#Device.description: A tangible object designed to accomplish a particular task in households, common public buildings or offices. In order to accomplish this task, the device performs one or more functions. For example, a washing machine is designed to wash (task) and to accomplish this task it performs a start and stop function. Devices can be structured in categories (subclasses) that reflect the different domain in which a device is used, e.g., smart appliances domain (subclass FunctionRelated) vs. building domain (subclass BuildingRelated) vs. smart grid domain (subclass EnergyRelated). New categories can be defined,if needed, to reflect other differences, for example different points of view, such as the point of view of the device's user vs. the point of view of the device's manufacturer. We propose a list of devices that are relevant for the purpose of SAREF, but this list can be extended.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: IndoorComfort
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Indoor comfort describes the indoor environment

https://w3id.org/sbeo#VisuallyImpairedPe.label: Visually Impaired Person
https://w3id.org/sbeo#VisuallyImpairedPe.description: It is a partial or total unability to see. It is also known as visual loss. Here, we considered a person having visual impairment who as severe low vision; 20/200 to 20/400 or higher.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#SensoryImpairedPer.label: Sensory Impairmed Person
https://w3id.org/sbeo#SensoryImpairedPer.description: Sensory impairment is when one of the senses such as sight, hearing, smell, touch, taste and spatial awareness, is no longer normal.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: Pipe
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The pipe in HVAC system, DHW system, etc.

https://w3id.org/saref#Light.label: Light
https://w3id.org/saref#Light.description: A saref:Property related to some measurements that are characterized by a certain value that is measured in a illuminance unit (lux)

https://w3id.org/sbeo#CorridorEntrance.label: Corridor Entrance
https://w3id.org/sbeo#CorridorEntrance.description: A logical point that is used to represent the entrance to a corridor.

https://w3id.org/seas/Greenhouse.label: Greenhouse
https://w3id.org/seas/Greenhouse.description: A building, room, or zone, usually chiefly of glass, in which the temperature is maintained within a desired range, used for cultivating tender plants or growing plants out of season.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#BuildingEntrance.label: Building Entrance
https://w3id.org/sbeo#BuildingEntrance.description: A logical point that is used to represent the entrance to a building.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Transp.label: Transport element
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Transp.description: A transport element is a generalization of all transport related objects that move people, animals or goods within a building or building complex. The TransportElement defines the occurrence of a transport element.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Intensity.label: Intensity
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Intensity.description: The measurable amount of a property, such as force, brightness, or a magnetic field (Oxford Dictionary of English).

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/TubeBu.description: A tube bundle is a device consisting of tubes and bundles of tubes used for heat transfer and contained typically within other energy conversion devices, such as a chiller or coil.

https://w3id.org/respond#ThermoSolarSyst.label: Thermo Solar System
https://w3id.org/respond#ThermoSolarSyst.description: A power system designed to supply thermal energy by means of solar energy.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#FireExtinguisher.description: An active fire protection instrument used by persons to blow out small scale fire.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#CinemaBuilding.label: Cinema Building
https://w3id.org/sbeo#CinemaBuilding.description: A building in which movies are shown.

https://w3id.org/saref#MicroRenewable.description: A device that generates renewable energy from natural resources such as teh sun, wind and water

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: EnergyCost
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: The cost for purchasing energy from external grid

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Outlet.label: Outlet
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Outlet.description:
An outlet is a device installed at a point to receive one or more inserted plugs for electrical power or communications.

Power outlets are commonly connected within a junction box; data outlets may be directly connected to a wall. For power outlets sharing the same circuit within a junction box, the ports should indicate the logical wiring relationship to the enclosing junction box, even though they may be physically connected to a cable going to another outlet, switch, or fixture.

https://w3id.org/saref#Commodity.label: Commodity
https://w3id.org/saref#Commodity.description: A marketable item for which there is demand, but which is supplied without qualitative differentiation across a market. SAREF refers to energy commodities such as electricity, gas, coal and oil.

https://w3id.org/seas/Bedroom.label: Bedroom
https://w3id.org/seas/Bedroom.description: Bedroom is used mainly for sleeping.

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: EnergyTariff
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: Tariff that is charged per energy unit by district energy providers

https://w3id.org/sbeo#DayCare.label: Day Care
https://w3id.org/sbeo#DayCare.description: A place where infants and young children are provided a supervision and care of during the daytime, particularly so that their parents can hold jobs.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/FlowSt.label: Flow storage device
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/FlowSt.description: The distribution flow element FlowStorageDevice defines the occurrence of a device that participates in a distribution system and is used for temporary storage (such as a tank).

http://www.w3.org/ns/sosa/Sensor.label: Sensor
http://www.w3.org/ns/sosa/Sensor.description: Device, agent (including humans), or software (simulation) involved in, or implementing, a Procedure. Sensors respond to a stimulus, e.g., a change in the environment, or input data composed from the results of prior Observations, and generate a Result. Sensors can be hosted by Platforms.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The generating unit produces energy with renewable resources.

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: The intensity of outdoor solar radiation

https://w3id.org/respond#DomesticColdWat.label: Domestic Cold Water Consumption
https://w3id.org/respond#DomesticColdWat.description: The amount of domestic cold water consumed.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#HallExit.label: Hall Exit
https://w3id.org/sbeo#HallExit.description: A logical point that is used to represent the exit of a Hall.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#RoomEntrance.label: Room Entrance
https://w3id.org/sbeo#RoomEntrance.description: A logical point that is used to represent the entrance to a room.

https://w3id.org/saref#SwitchOnService.label: Switch on service
https://w3id.org/saref#SwitchOnService.description: A type of service that represents an on/off function to the network

https://w3id.org/saref#Command.label: Command
https://w3id.org/saref#Command.description: A directive that a device must support to perform a certain function. A command may act upon a state, but does not necessarily act upon a state. For example, the ON command acts upon the ON/OFF state, but the GET command does not act upon any state, it simply gives a directive to retrieve a certain value. We propose here a list of commands that are relevant for the purpose of SAREF, but this list can be extended.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#HorizontalPassage.description: A type of a passage that connects spatial elements located on the same storey or floor.

https://saref.etsi.org/core/Property.label: Property
https://saref.etsi.org/core/Property.description: An aspect of an entity that can be observable by a sensor.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#ShoppingMallBuildi.description: A set of one or more buildings forming a complex of shops representing merchandisers, with interconnecting walkways enabling visitors to easily walk from unit to unit, along with a parking area.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#SeatingArrangement.label: Seating Arrangement Route
https://w3id.org/sbeo#SeatingArrangement.description: A type of route in which the ending point represents the seat of one or more individuals such as in cinema, stadiums, auditoriums, etc.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#FireEscapeLadder.label: Fire Escape Ladder
https://w3id.org/sbeo#FireEscapeLadder.description: A type of ladder which is used to make an emergency exit through the window out of fire accident site.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Visit.label: Visit
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Visit.description: An activity that represents an act of going to see a person or place as a guest, tourist or a spectator. Normally, this type of activity is considered as a leasiure or recreational activity.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#HandheldDevice.label: Handheld Device
https://w3id.org/sbeo#HandheldDevice.description: A smart device or a computer that is small enough to be used while holding it in the hands.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: Stakeholder
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The stakeholder in district or building that has performance goals to be achieved

https://w3id.org/sbeo#LowStaminaPerson.description: It is the unability to sustain physical or mental activity to a normal level.

http://purl.oclc.org/NET/ssnx/ssn#Observ.label: ObservationValue
http://purl.oclc.org/NET/ssnx/ssn#Observ.description: The observation result of the sensor or meter

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Exit.label: Exit

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: BuildingParameter
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: The building static parameter that to be measured, like area, volume, material property

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Coil.label: Coil
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Coil.description:
A coil is a device used to provide heat transfer between non-mixing media. A common example is a cooling coil, which utilizes a finned coil in which circulates chilled water, antifreeze, or refrigerant that is used to remove heat from air moving across the surface of the coil. A coil may be used either for heating or cooling purposes by placing a series of tubes (the coil) carrying a heating or cooling fluid into an airstream. The coil may be constructed from tubes bundled in a serpentine form or from finned tubes that give a extended heat transfer surface.

Coils may also be used for non-airflow cases such as embedded in a floor slab.

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.label: Timestamp
https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: A timestamp is a sequence of characters or encoded information identifying when a certain event occurred, usually giving the date and time of day, sometimes accurate to a small fraction of a second.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/AirToA.label: Air to air heat recovery
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/AirToA.description: An air-to-air heat recovery device employs a counter-flow heat exchanger between inbound and outbound air flow. It is typically used to transfer heat from warmer air in one chamber to cooler air in the second chamber (i.e., typically used to recover heat from the conditioned air being exhausted and the outside air being supplied to a building), resulting in energy savings from reduced heating (or cooling) requirements.

http://identifiers.org/mamo/Mathematical.label: Mathematical_model
http://identifiers.org/mamo/Mathematical.description: The mathematical model for KPI calculation. The model is composed of a set of variables and a set of equations that establish relationships between the variables.

https://w3id.org/respond#VOC.label: VOC
https://w3id.org/respond#VOC.description: A property that determines the Volatile Organic Compounds (VOC).

https://w3id.org/sbeo#PointOfInterestSeg.label: Point Of Interest Segement
https://w3id.org/sbeo#PointOfInterestSeg.description: A traverasal part of a space that is connected to a specific Point of Interest.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Evapor.label: Evaporator
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Evapor.description: An evaporator is a device in which a liquid refrigerent is vaporized and absorbs heat from the surrounding fluid.

http://identifiers.org/mamo/Dependent_va.label: Dependent_variable

https://smartdatamodels.org/dataModel.En.label: AC Meausrement
https://smartdatamodels.org/dataModel.En.description: The Data Model intended to measure the electrical energies consumed by an electrical system which uses an Alternating Current (AC) for a three-phase (L1, L2, L3) or single-phase (L) and neutral (N). It integrates the initial version of the data Modem [THREEPHASEMEASUREMENT], extended to also perform single-phase measurements. It includes attributes for various electrical measurements such as power, frequency, current and voltage.

https://w3id.org/respond#OnOffProperty.label: On Off
https://w3id.org/respond#OnOffProperty.description: A property that determines if something is activated (on) or not (off).

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Vibrat.label: Vibration isolator
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Vibrat.description: A vibration isolator is a device used to minimize the effects of vibration transmissibility in a building.

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: EnergyCostTariff
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: The tariff for purchasing energy per unit from main grid (district energy provider)

https://saref.etsi.org/core/Measurement.label: Measurement
https://saref.etsi.org/core/Measurement.description: Represents the measured value made over a property. It is also linked to the unit of measure in which the value is expressed and the timestamp of the measurement.

https://w3id.org/saref#StopState.label: Stop state
https://w3id.org/saref#StopState.description: The state of a device that is STOPPED

https://w3id.org/saref#Function.label: Function
https://w3id.org/saref#Function.description: The functionality necessary to accomplish the task for which a Device is designed. A device can be designed to perform more than one function. Functions can be structured in categories (subclasses) that reflect different points of view, for example, considering the specific application area for which a function can be used (e.g., light, temperature, motion, heat, power, etc.), or the capability that a function can support (e.g., receive, reply, notify, etc.), and so forth.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Generating unit is the power unit that generates energy for district end users, like photovoltaic array.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Electr.label: Electric generator
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Electr.description: An electric generator is an engine that is a machine for converting mechanical energy into electrical energy.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#AvailabilityStatus.label: Availability Status
https://w3id.org/sbeo#AvailabilityStatus.description: It expresses the status of something that is either available or not.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: DHWSystem
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The domestic hot water system that provides hot water to the building

https://w3id.org/sbeo#PersonAbility.label: Person Ability
https://w3id.org/sbeo#PersonAbility.description: A parameter to express the quality of an ability a specific person (or type of persons) own.

http://identifiers.org/mamo/Variable.label: Variable
http://identifiers.org/mamo/Variable.description: The variable in the mathematical model of KPI calculation

https://w3id.org/respond#IT.label: IT
https://w3id.org/respond#IT.description: An appliance related to IT (Information Technology) such as computer, printer, etc.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#ExitRoute.label: Exit Route
https://w3id.org/sbeo#ExitRoute.description: A type of route in which the ending point represents the exit of a specific space.

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: A compressor is a mechanical device that increases the pressure of a gas by reducing its volume.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#DeviationState.label: Deviation State
https://w3id.org/sbeo#DeviationState.description: It models the user's deviation state from one's proposed route. For example, NoDeviate, RareDeviate, OftenDeviate, and TooOftenDeviate.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: EnergyProduction
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Energy producted by generation system

https://w3id.org/seas/Property.label: Property
https://w3id.org/seas/Property.description: Property is a system of rights that gives people legal control of valuable things, and also refers to the valuable things themselves.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Role.label: Role
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Role.description: The function assumed or part played by a person or thing in a particular situation (Oxford Dictionary of English).

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Junction.label: Junction
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Junction.description: A logic point that connects more than two corridors.

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: EnergyProducerFacility
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: The energy facility in the building that produces energy

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Navigation.label: Navigation
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Navigation.description: The process or activity of accurately ascertaining one's position and planning and following a route (Oxford Dictionary of English).

https://w3id.org/seas/UtilityRoom.label: Utility Room
https://w3id.org/seas/UtilityRoom.description: Room for other special utilities and hobbies.

https://w3id.org/saref#Storage.label: Storage
https://w3id.org/saref#Storage.description: A type of energy-related device that stores energy

https://w3id.org/seas/Basement.label: Basement
https://w3id.org/seas/Basement.description: A story of a building, partly or wholly underground.

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: BuildingThing
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: All things (here physical objects) that creates a building, it is intended as root of all building components.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Tank.label: Tank
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Tank.description: A tank is a vessel or container in which a fluid or gas is stored for later use.

https://w3id.org/saref#Temperature.label: Temperature
https://w3id.org/saref#Temperature.description: A saref:Property related to some measurements that are characterized by a certain value that is measured in a temperature unit (degree_Celsius, degree_Fahrenheit, or degree_kelvin)

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.label: Humidifier
https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: A humidifier is a household appliance or device designed to increase the moisture level in the air within a room or an enclosed space.

https://w3id.org/seas/SiteOfBuilding.label: Site Of Building
https://w3id.org/seas/SiteOfBuilding.description: Building site is a locale containing one or more separate buildings. They are zones.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Compre.label: Compressor
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Compre.description: A compressor is a device that compresses a fluid typically used in a refrigeration circuit.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#KidsArea.label: Kids Area
https://w3id.org/sbeo#KidsArea.description: A place where infants or young children can play which is surrounded by a boundary normally. It can be both indoor and outdoor.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: EquipmentContainer
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Equipment container is the district power unit that contains groups of equipment, like substation, plant, Distributed Energy Resources, etc.

https://w3id.org/saref#Price.label: Price
https://w3id.org/saref#Price.description: A saref:Property crelated to some measurements that are characterized by a certain value that is measured using saref:Currency

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Electr.label: Electric motor
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Electr.description: An electric motor is an engine that is a machine for converting electrical energy into mechanical energy.

https://w3id.org/saref#Actuator.label: Actuator
https://w3id.org/saref#Actuator.description: A device responsible for moving or controlling a mechanism or system by performing an actuating function

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Damper.label: Damper
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Damper.description: A damper typically participates in an HVAC duct distribution system and is used to control or modulate the flow of air.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Cooled.label: Cooled beam
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Cooled.description: A cooled beam (or chilled beam) is a device typically used to cool air by circulating a fluid such as chilled water through exposed finned tubes above a space. Typically mounted overhead near or within a ceiling, the cooled beam uses convection to cool the space below it by acting as a heat sink for the naturally rising warm air of the space. Once cooled, the air naturally drops back to the floor where the cycle begins again.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#AssemblyPoint.label: Assembly Point
https://w3id.org/sbeo#AssemblyPoint.description: A place where people in an office, etc. should go if there is an emergency, for example, a fire (Cambridge English Dictionary) .

https://w3id.org/seas/Kitchen.label: Kitchen
https://w3id.org/seas/Kitchen.description: Kitchen is a room used mainly for cooking and possibly eating.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#AutonomousMobility.label: Autonomous Mobility Person
https://w3id.org/sbeo#AutonomousMobility.description: A person who can walk on his/her with a help of a non-motorised device, e.g. crutches.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Family.label: Family
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Family.description: A group of two or more persons having family ties between them.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: EnergyConsumer
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Energy consumer is the power unit that consumes energy in district microgrid.

https://w3id.org/saref#SetAbsoluteLevelC.label: Set absolute level command
https://w3id.org/saref#SetAbsoluteLevelC.description: A type of set level command

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/AudioV.label: Audio visual appliance
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/AudioV.description:
An audio-visual appliance is a device that displays, captures, transmits, or receives audio or video.

Audio-visual appliances may be fixed in place or may be able to be moved from one space to another. They may require an electrical supply that may be supplied either by an electrical circuit or provided from a local battery source. Audio-visual appliances may be connected to data circuits including specialist circuits for audio visual purposes only.

https://w3id.org/respond#TumbleDryer.label: Tumble Dryer
https://w3id.org/respond#TumbleDryer.description: A machine that dries washed clothes by spinning them in hot air inside a rotating drum.

https://w3id.org/saref#EnergyMeter.label: Energy meter
https://w3id.org/saref#EnergyMeter.description: An energy meter is a device of category saref:Meter that consists of a meter, accomplishes the tasks saref:MeterReading and saref:EnergyEfficiency, performs the saref:MeteringFunction and is used for the purpose of measuring the saref:Energy property

https://w3id.org/seas/BuildingSpace.label: Building Space
https://w3id.org/seas/BuildingSpace.description: A Space is a 3D volume bounded by surfaces. It represents the physical spaces of the building.

https://w3id.org/seas/MercantileBuilding.label: Mercantile Building
https://w3id.org/seas/MercantileBuilding.description: Places where goods are displayed and sold. Examples: grocery stores, department stores, and gas stations.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#MobilityWithAssist.label: Mobility With Assisted Device Person
https://w3id.org/sbeo#MobilityWithAssist.description: A person who used a motorised device for moving, such as motorised wheelchair or scooter, etc.

https://w3id.org/seas/Building.label: Building
https://w3id.org/seas/Building.description: Buildings are roofed constructions which can be used separately, have been built for permanent purposes, can be entered by persons and are suitable or intended for protecting persons, animals or objects.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#IncidentProtection.label: Incident Protection Device
https://w3id.org/sbeo#IncidentProtection.description: Device that is used to avoid and minimize the effects of potentially danger events for the occupants of the building as well as for the structure of building itself.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Humidi.label: Humidifier
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Humidi.description: A humidifier is a device that adds moisture into the air.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Transf.label: Transformer
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Transf.description:
A transformer is an inductive stationary device that transfers electrical energy from one circuit to another.

Transformer is used to transform electric power; conversion of electric signals for other purposes is handled at other entities: Controller converts arbitrary signals, AudioVisualAppliance converts signals for audio or video streams, and CommunicationsAppliance converts signals for data or other communications usage.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Buildi.label: Building device
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Buildi.description: A tangible object designed to accomplish a particular task in a building.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Panic.label: Panic
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Panic.description: A  sudden uncontrollable fear or anxiety among the occupants of the building that often causes wildly unthinking behaviour (Oxford Dictionary of English). In indoor environments, it may happens due to several reasons such as earthquake, fire or other sudden hazardous situations.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#VisitingGroup.label: Visiting Group
https://w3id.org/sbeo#VisitingGroup.description: A group of two or more people that has the same objective of visiting any space.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#PersonAccessibilit.label: Person Accessibility
https://w3id.org/sbeo#PersonAccessibilit.description: A parameter to express the accessibility of any space for a specific person (or type of persons) in terms of some value.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#TextualDescription.label: Textual Description
https://w3id.org/sbeo#TextualDescription.description: A type of description in which only text or writings are used to provide the information about any entity.

http://www.ontologydesignpatterns.org/on.label: PhysicalObject
http://www.ontologydesignpatterns.org/on.description: An inanimate, three-dimensional object or substance. The physical object identified in the district.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Space.label: Space
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Space.description: A physical, three dimensional, and one of the most important elements in the design of architecture that can further be divided to create more spaces from it.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Boiler.label: Boiler
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Boiler.description: A boiler is a closed, pressure-rated vessel in which water or other fluid is heated using an energy source such as natural gas, heating oil, or electricity. The fluid in the vessel is then circulated out of the boiler for use in various processes or heating applications.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Parameter.label: Parameter
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Parameter.description: A numerical or other measurable factor forming one of a set that defines a system or sets the conditions of its operation.

https://w3id.org/saref#EventFunction.label: Event function
https://w3id.org/saref#EventFunction.description: A function that allows to notify another device that a certain threshold value has been exceeded.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: WindDirection
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The weather phenomenon of wind direction

https://w3id.org/sbeo#FireProtectionDevi.label: Fire Protection Device
https://w3id.org/sbeo#FireProtectionDevi.description: Device that is used to minimize the effects of potentially danger fires.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Coolin.label: Cooling tower
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Coolin.description: A cooling tower is a device which rejects heat to ambient air by circulating a fluid such as water through it to reduce its temperature by partial evaporation.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: TacticalKPI
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The tactical KPI is the KPI at tactical decision level, the KPI disaggregated from strategic KPI corresponding to building and system level

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: Fan
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The fan in HVAC system

http://purl.org/ontology/olo/core#Slot.label: Slot
http://purl.org/ontology/olo/core#Slot.description: A slot in an ordered list with a fixed index.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: ExteriorWindow
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The exterior window that contributes thermal load to the building and zone

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: EnvironmentalParameter
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: The parameter describes the environment condition, including indoor environment (indoor comfort) and outdoor environment (weather)

https://w3id.org/seas/Laundry.label: Laundry
https://w3id.org/seas/Laundry.description: A room or zone, as in a home or apartment building, reserved for doing the family wash.

https://w3id.org/saref#OffState.label: Off state
https://w3id.org/saref#OffState.description: The state of a device that is ON

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.label: Pump
https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: A pump is a device that moves fluids, or sometimes slurries, by mechanical action, typically converted from electrical energy into hydraulic or pneumatic...

https://w3id.org/sbeo#GraphBasedRoute.label: Graph-Based Route
https://w3id.org/sbeo#GraphBasedRoute.description: A graph-based route is a graphical way of representing a route in terms of nodes (sometimes called vertices) and edges (sometimes called arcs).

https://w3id.org/sbeo#VisitRoute.label: Visit Route
https://w3id.org/sbeo#VisitRoute.description: A type of route created for visiting purposes within an indoor environment.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#SevereHearingImpai.label: Severe Hearing Impaired Person
https://w3id.org/sbeo#SevereHearingImpai.description: A person who is unable to hear anything without wearing a hearing aid.

https://w3id.org/respond#PhotovoltaicSys.label: Photovoltaic System
https://w3id.org/respond#PhotovoltaicSys.description: A power system designed to supply usable solar power by means of photovoltaics.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#MotorisedWheelchai.label: Motorised Wheelchair Person
https://w3id.org/sbeo#MotorisedWheelchai.description: It is a kind of wheelchair that has either a mechanical engine or an electric battery. It has more speed than a traditional wheelchair.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Thoroughfare.label: Thoroughfare
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Thoroughfare.description: A road or path forming a route between two places (Oxford Dictionary of English).

https://w3id.org/sbeo#ActiveFireProtecti.label: Active Fire Protection Device
https://w3id.org/sbeo#ActiveFireProtecti.description: It is an integral part of the fire protection used as a first response to minimize the spread of fire and related effects.

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: WeatherPhenomenon
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: Weather phenomenon describes the ourdoor temperature, humidity, solar radiation and wind.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: ExteriorWall
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The exterior wall that contributes thermal load to the building and zone

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: Occupant
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The occupant who occupies the zone in building

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.label: Flow
https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: A property related to measurements of rates of flow, measured in m³/s. This may for instance be used to measure air flow in ventilation or water flow in district heating systems.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#StadiumBuilding.label: Stadium Building
https://w3id.org/sbeo#StadiumBuilding.description: A building that has a large open area surrounded by many rows of seats, and  used for sports events, concerts, etc.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Filter.label: Filter
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Filter.description: A filter is an apparatus used to remove particulate or gaseous matter from fluids and gases.

https://w3id.org/respond#StoveOven.label: Stove Oven
https://w3id.org/respond#StoveOven.description: A piece of equipment that burns fuel or uses electricity in order to heat a place.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: ThermalComfort
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Thermal comfort describes the comfort condition related indoor temperature and humidity

https://w3id.org/saref#Motion.label: Motion
https://w3id.org/saref#Motion.description: A saref:Property related to some measurements that are characterized by a certain value that is measured in a unit of measure for motion

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.label: Chiller
https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: A chiller is a machine that removes heat from a liquid coolant via a vapor-compression, adsorption refrigeration, or absorption refrigeration cycles.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: VisualComfort
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The visual comfort describes the comfort related to light condition

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/FlowMe.label: Flow meter
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/FlowMe.description: A flow meter is a device that is used to measure the flow rate in a system.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: ElectricVehicle
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Electric vehicle that stores energy.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/HeatEx.label: Heat exchanger
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/HeatEx.description: A heat exchanger is a device used to provide heat transfer between non-mixing media such as plate and shell and tube heat exchangers. HeatExchanger is commonly used on water-side distribution systems to recover energy from a liquid to another liquid (typically water-based), whereas AirToAirHeatRecovery is commonly used on air-side distribution systems to recover energy from a gas to a gas (usually air).

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Valve.label: Valve
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Valve.description: A valve is used in a building services piping distribution system to control or modulate the flow of the fluid.

http://www.w3.org/2006/time#Instant.label: Instant
http://www.w3.org/2006/time#Instant.description: An instant is a time instant

https://w3id.org/sbeo#ChemicalSpill.label: Chemical Spill
https://w3id.org/sbeo#ChemicalSpill.description: A hazardous event that happens due to the spillage or leakge of any toxic chemical in the building.

http://schema.org/Event.label: Event
http://schema.org/Event.description: The event taken place in the zone, such entertainment event, dinner, conference, etc. A non-persistent, time-based occurrence, has location, duration, and responsible actors associated with an event.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Unitar.label: Unitary control element
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Unitar.description:
A unitary control element combines a number of control components into a single product, such as a thermostat or humidistat.

A unitary control element provides a housing for an aggregation of control or electrical distribution elements that, in combination, perform a singular (unitary) purpose. Each item in the aggregation may have its own geometric representation and location.

https://w3id.org/saref#Pressure.label: Pressure
https://w3id.org/saref#Pressure.description: A saref:Property related to some measurements that are characterized by a certain value that is measured in a pressure unit (bar or pascal)

https://w3id.org/sbeo#TerroristAttack.label: Terrorist Attack
https://w3id.org/sbeo#TerroristAttack.description: A surprise event involving the deliberate use of violence against the occupants of the buildings.

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.label: Building related
https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: A building represents a structure that provides shelter for its occupants or contents and stands in one place. The building is also used to provide a basic element within the spatial structure hierarchy for the components of a building project (together with site, storey, and space).

https://w3id.org/saref#CloseState.label: Close state
https://w3id.org/saref#CloseState.description: The state of a device that is CLOSE

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Group.label: Group
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Group.description: It represents a collection of two or more people who interact with each other and may have the similar characteristics or objectives.

https://w3id.org/seas/HomeOffice.label: Tree house
https://w3id.org/seas/HomeOffice.description: A work or office space set up in a person's home and used exclusively for business on a regular basis.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Engine.label: Engine
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Engine.description: An engine is a device that converts fuel into mechanical energy through combustion.

https://w3id.org/respond#TV.label: TV
https://w3id.org/respond#TV.description: A device shaped like a box with a screen that receives electrical signals and changes them into moving images and sound, or the method or business of sending images and sound by electrical signals.

https://w3id.org/seas/Door.label: Door
https://w3id.org/seas/Door.description: the large flat piece of wood, glass etc that you move when you go into or out of a building, room, vehicle etc, or when you open a cupboard (Longman Dictionary of Contemporary English Online)

https://w3id.org/saref#Currency.label: Currency unit
https://w3id.org/saref#Currency.description: The unit of measure for price

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: WindGeneratingUnit
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The generating unit produces energy with wind energy, wind turbines.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#RoomExit.label: Room Exit
https://w3id.org/sbeo#RoomExit.description: A logical point that is used to represent the exit of a room.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: KPICalculation
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The KPI calculation to calculate specific KPI with mathematical model and datum source

https://w3id.org/respond#Gateway.label: Gateway
https://w3id.org/respond#Gateway.description: A gateway is a piece of networking hardware used in telecommunications for telecommunications networks that allows data to flow from one discrete network to another.

http://www.w3.org/2006/time#TemporalEnti.label: TemporalEntity
http://www.w3.org/2006/time#TemporalEnti.description: A temporal entity is an instant or an interval

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: BuildingEquipment
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The equipment contained in building

https://w3id.org/seas/ResidentialBuildin.label: Residential Building
https://w3id.org/seas/ResidentialBuildin.description: A residential building is a building at least half of which is used for residential purposes.

https://w3id.org/saref#DoorSwitch.label: Door switch
https://w3id.org/saref#DoorSwitch.description: A device of category saref:Actuator that consists of a switch, accomplishes the task saref:Safety, performs the saref:OpenCloseFunction, is used for controlling a door, and can be found in the state saref:OpenCloseState.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#HorizontalMovingPa.label: Horizontal Moving Pavement
https://w3id.org/sbeo#HorizontalMovingPa.description: A slow-moving conveyor mechanism that transports people across a horizontal plane over a short to medium distance.

https://w3id.org/respond#Icon.label: Icon
https://w3id.org/respond#Icon.description: A small picture or symbol that represents a resource.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#MentalAbility.label: Mental Ability
https://w3id.org/sbeo#MentalAbility.description: The mental ability of an individual to remain focused on an external stimuli or an internal experience for a certain period of time (World Health Organisation).

https://w3id.org/saref#LevelControlFunct.label: Level control function
https://w3id.org/saref#LevelControlFunct.description: An actuating function that allows to do level adjustments of an actuator in a certain range (e.g., 0%-100%), such as dimming a light or set the speed of an electric motor.

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.label: Voltage
https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: Voltage in Volts

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Ramp.label: Ramp
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Ramp.description: A sloping surface joining two different levels, as at the entrance or between floors of a building (Oxford Dictionary of English).

https://w3id.org/sbeo#OpenAreaExit.label: Open Area Exit
https://w3id.org/sbeo#OpenAreaExit.description: A logical point that is used to represent the exit from a specifc open space.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: Roof
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: The roof of the zone

https://w3id.org/saref#SensingFunction.label: Sensing function
https://w3id.org/saref#SensingFunction.description: A function that allows to transmit data from sensors, such as measurement values (e.g., temperature) or sensing data (e.g., occupancy)

https://w3id.org/sbeo#AlertingDevice.label: Alerting Device
https://w3id.org/sbeo#AlertingDevice.description: Alerting devices are those devices that are used to react to normal as well as alarming situations to let people know that some specific condition is occuring.

https://w3id.org/saref#Property.label: Property
https://w3id.org/saref#Property.description: Anything that can be sensed, measured or controlled in households, common public buildings or offices. We propose here a list of properties that are relevant for the purpose of SAREF, but this list can be extended.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#MobilityWithWheelc.label: Mobility With Wheelchair Person
https://w3id.org/sbeo#MobilityWithWheelc.description: A person using a wheelchair (a mobility device that is used while having any kind of tempoary or permanent physical impairment).

http://www.w3.org/ns/sosa/ObservableProp.label: Observable Property
http://www.w3.org/ns/sosa/ObservableProp.description: An observable quality (property, characteristic) of a FeatureOfInterest.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/FlowCo.label: Flow controller
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/FlowCo.description: The distribution flow element FlowController defines the occurrence of elements of a distribution system that are used to regulate flow through a distribution system. Examples include dampers, valves, switches, and relays.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#EndPoint.label: End Point
https://w3id.org/sbeo#EndPoint.description: A logic point that is used to indicate the end of a corridor.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Fan.label: Fan
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Fan.description: A fan is a device which imparts mechanical work on a gas. A typical usage of a fan is to induce airflow in a building services air distribution system.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Alarm.label: Alarm
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Alarm.description:
An alarm is a device that signals the existence of a condition or situation that is outside the boundaries of normal expectation or that activates such a device.

Alarms include the provision of break glass buttons and manual pull boxes that are used to activate alarms.

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.label: Tenant
https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: A class that describes information about a tenant in a building.

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Chille.label: Chiller
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Chille.description: A chiller is a device used to remove heat from a liquid via a vapor-compression or absorption refrigeration cycle to cool a fluid, typically water or a mixture of water and glycol. The chilled fluid is then used to cool and dehumidify air in a building.

https://w3id.org/respond#CommonArea.label: Common Area
https://w3id.org/respond#CommonArea.description: Amenities such as corridors, hallways, lobby, pool, toilets provided for the comfort and use of all occupants, owners, tenants, or users of a building or building complex.

https://w3id.org/respond#AirConditioner.label: Air Conditioner
https://w3id.org/respond#AirConditioner.description: A machine that keeps the air in a building cool.

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: BuildingElement
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: The element composes the building structure.

http://identifiers.org/mamo/Independent_.label: Independent_variable
http://identifiers.org/mamo/Independent_.description: A variable is considered dependent if it depends on an independent variable.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#SocialUnit.label: Social Unit
https://w3id.org/sbeo#SocialUnit.description: An individual, or a group or community, considered as a discrete constituent of a society or larger group (Oxford Dictionary of English).

https://w3id.org/sbeo#EventSeverity.label: Event Severity
https://w3id.org/sbeo#EventSeverity.description: A parameter to express the severity of an event for a specific person (or type of persons) in terms of  potential severity levels.

https://w3id.org/saref#OpenState.label: Open state
https://w3id.org/saref#OpenState.description: The state of a device that is OPEN

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: EnergyConsumerFacility
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: The energy facility in the building that consumes energy.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: WindPowerSystem
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: System that generates electricity through wind power

https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Interc.label: Interceptor
https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Interc.description: An interceptor is a device designed and installed in order to separate and retain deleterious, hazardous or undesirable matter while permitting normal sewage or liquids to discharge into a collection system by gravity.

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: EnergyGain
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: The energy gain is the produced energy that feed into external grid so as to obtain monetary gain

https://w3id.org/respond#DayType.label: Day Type
https://w3id.org/respond#DayType.description: Represents the type of weather for a given location (e.g. sunny, cloudy,...).

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Device.label: Device
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Device.description: A thing made or adapted for a particular purpose, especially a piece of mechanical or electronic equipment (Oxford Dictionary of English).

https://w3id.org/respond#Boiler.label: Boiler
https://w3id.org/respond#Boiler.description: A device that heats water, especially to provide heating and hot water in a house.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Activity.label: Activity
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Activity.description: A process that a person, group of persons, or machine does during a specific amount of time, such as visiting, shopping, etc.

https://w3id.org/saref#Measurement.label: Measurement
https://w3id.org/saref#Measurement.description: Represents the measured value made over a property. It is also linked to the unit of measure in which the value is expressed and the timestamp of the measurement.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#SmartPhone.label: Smartphone
https://w3id.org/sbeo#SmartPhone.description: A mobile phone that is able to perform many of the functions of a computer, typically having a relatively large screen and an operating system capable of running general-purpose applications (Oxford Dictionary of English).

https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.label: Volume
https://sargon-n5geh.netlify.app/ontolog.description: A property related to measurements measured in volume. Volume is measured in m³.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Telephone.label: Telephone
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Telephone.description: A device for transmitting voices over a distance using wire or radio, by converting acoustic vibrations to electrical signals (Oxford Dictionary of English).

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: BuildingType
http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.description: Building type describes the functinonal type of a building

https://w3id.org/saref#OnOffFunction.label: On off function
https://w3id.org/saref#OnOffFunction.description: An actuating function that allows to switch on and off an actuator

https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.label: EnergyDemand
https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/.description: The energy demand in district or buildings

https://w3id.org/sbeo#VerticalPassage.label: Vertical Passage
https://w3id.org/sbeo#VerticalPassage.description: A type of passage that connects spatial elements located on different storey or floor.

https://saref.etsi.org/core/UnitOfMeasur.label: Unit of measure
https://saref.etsi.org/core/UnitOfMeasur.description: The unit of measure is a standard for measurement of a quantity, such as a Property. For example,  Power is a property and Watt is a unit of power that represents a definite predetermined power: when we say 10 Watt, we actually mean 10 times the definite predetermined power called "watt". Our definition is based on the definition of unit of measure in the Ontology of units of Measure (OM). We propose here a list of some units of measure that are relevant for the purpose of the Smart Appliances ontology, but this list can be extended.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Road.label: Road
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Road.description: A road is a way on land between two places that has been paved or otherwise improved to allow travel by foot or some form of conveyance, including a motor vehicle, cart, bicycle, or horse.

https://w3id.org/sbeo#Severity.label: Severity
https://w3id.org/sbeo#Severity.description: The quality of being hard to endure, uninviting or formidable.

http://energy.linkeddata.es/em-kpi/ontol.label: PerformanceGoal

A communications appliance transmits and receives electronic or digital information as data or sound.

Communication appliances may be fixed in place or may be able to be moved from one space to another. Communication appliances require an electrical supply that may be supplied either by an electrical circuit or provided from a local battery source.
